seoTitle: Create a Project Schedule in Smartsheet with Gantt
metaDescription: Build a project schedule in Smartsheet with task hierarchies, the Dependencies Engine, predecessor links, and a live Gantt Chart view.
intro: A solid project schedule needs more than a list of dates, it needs dependencies and visual tracking that adjust automatically. This guide covers structuring your schedule sheet, enabling the Dependencies Engine, linking predecessor tasks, and configuring the Gantt Chart view with status indicators.

steps.heading: Set up your master schedule sheet
steps.body: Create your core tracking columns: Task Name, Start Date, End Date, Duration, and Predecessors.

steps.heading: Build a task hierarchy
steps.body: Use indent and outdent controls to create a clean parent-child structure representing project phases.

steps.heading: Enable the Dependencies Engine
steps.body: Turn on dependencies in your sheet settings so timelines shift automatically and the critical path is tracked.

steps.heading: Link tasks with Predecessors
steps.body: Map task connections in the Predecessors column so downstream milestones adjust when delays happen.

steps.heading: Configure the Gantt Chart view
steps.body: Activate and style the Gantt Chart to visually present your timeline, and set up RYG status balls to flag overdue milestones.

faq.question: Does Smartsheet automatically shift dates when a task is delayed?
faq.answer: Yes, once the Dependencies Engine is enabled and tasks are linked through Predecessors, downstream dates adjust automatically.

faq.question: How do I organize tasks into project phases?
faq.answer: Use indent and outdent controls to build a parent-child hierarchy representing each phase and its subtasks.

faq.question: What are RYG status indicators?
faq.answer: They're red, yellow, green balls used as visual status markers to help you spot overdue or at-risk milestones at a glance.

faq.question: Can I see my schedule as a Gantt chart?
faq.answer: Yes, the Gantt Chart View can be activated and styled directly from your sheet to visualize the full timeline.
